title: Quadlet service units do not report health properly anymore (5.6.2 -> 5.7.1)
body:
### Issue Description

`.container` units fail with `Notify=healthy` but are fine when it is commented out. `systemd` times them out, presumably it never receives the "ready" notification. Started happening with Fedora 43's 5.7.1 (had been on Fedora 41's 5.6.2; skipped over rebooting into Fedora 42).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I have this `.container` file:

```systemd
[Unit]
Description=Container for nginx in the DOMAIN pod
After=DOMAIN-letsencrypt.service

[Quadlet]

[Container]
Image=localhost/nginx:latest
AutoUpdate=local
CgroupsMode=no-conmon
Pod=DOMAIN.pod
ReadOnly=true
ReadOnlyTmpfs=false
Tmpfs=/var/tmp
Tmpfs=/var/log/journal
Tmpfs=/var/lib/systemd
Volume=<masked out>
Tmpfs=/var/lib/nginx/tmp
#Notify=healthy
HealthCmd=systemctl is-active nginx.service
HealthInterval=10s
HealthRetries=9
HealthOnFailure=kill
```

It starts up fine. If I uncomment `Notify=healthy`, it ends up timing out. However, I can see that `nginx.service` is fine inside the container.

### Describe the results you received

`systemd` times out the resulting `.service` unit due to a lack of ready notification.

### Describe the results you expected

`systemd` only considers the service "done" when the service(s) inside are actually ready.

### podman info output

```yaml
No SSH key handy; can get if needed.

podman-5.7.1-1.fc43.x86_64
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

DigitalOcean

### Additional information

Started happening after updating from Fedora 41 to Fedora 43 on the host. Did not boot into a Fedora 42 environment (but did `dnf upgrade --releasever=` through it).

comments.body:
Is this the actual line in your `.container` file:
```
HealthCmd=systemctl is-active nginx.service
```

The reason I'm asking is that this should not have worked to being with. Health commands are executed inside the container and as a result this command should have failed.

Have you tried replacing the health command with a simple `true`:
```
HealthCmd=true
```
This will allow us to understand if the root cause is the handling and propagation of the command's result or the result itself. 

I've tried it with both `5.6.2` and `5.7.1` and in both cases using `true` worked.
I've not tried replacing it yet, but the command is supposed to run inside the container as it has a `systemd` set up to do the initialization (and `.timer` stuff).

I see. Since I don't have your container image, I can't comment on its internal. If you want (and can) share the `Containerfile`, I might be able to take a look.

Another thing to check, with the commented line (that is the container keeps running), if you exec into the container, does `systemctl is-active nginx.service` return successfully?

I also see that this container is part of a pod. Can you share the complete configuration. Maybe it'll help spot something.

I tried starting containers without the pod and it was still being sad about it.

The containers are built with `buildah` (to avoid layer wastage). I'll try and get the simplest of them here (I *think* all secrets are volume-mounted in, but I want to make sure first).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
